q: Based on the diagram given below, which animal tissue would be selected for the lower jaw of a fish?
Which of the following statements about complex permanent tissue is correct?
o: Xylem and phloem are complex tissues that help in conduction and form vascular bundles.
Complex tissues are made of identical cells performing different functions.
Complex tissues are only found in animals for transporting oxygen.
Complex tissues consist of loosely packed parenchyma cells for storage.

q: In sexually reproducing organisms, how many versions of each trait does a child inherit?
o: Two versions from the mother only

q: Three resistors of 4 Ω, 8 Ω, and 12 Ω are connected in series across a 12 V battery. The current in the circuit is:
Washing soda is prepared by heating which compound?
Which of the following observations from Rutherford's alpha-particle scattering experiment led to the conclusion that most of the atom's volume is empty?
o: Most of the alpha particles passed straight through the gold foil.
Alpha particles were attracted towards electrons in the atom.
A few alpha particles were deflected by 180 degrees.
Some alpha particles were deflected by small angles.

q: Which of the following statements is true?
o: Power and work are independent of each other.
More work in less time means more power.
Work done does not affect power.

q: Which of the following is an advantage of vegetative propagation?
o: Plants grown this way take longer to bear fruits.
All new plants are genetically different from the parent.
It allows propagation of seedless plants such as banana.

q: What type of solution causes water to move out of a cell through the plasma membrane, making the cell shrink?
Which of the following statements is correct about the force?
o: Force is a scalar quantity measured in Joule.
Force is a scalar quantity measured in Newton.
Force is a vector quantity measured in Joule.
Force is a vector quantity measured in Newton.

q: The frequency of a sound wave is 250 Hz. What is its time period?
Which of the following statements about the Archimedes' Principle is correct?
o: When a body is immersed fully or partially in a fluid, it experiences an upward force that is much less than the weight of the fluid displaced by it.
When a body is immersed fully or partially in a fluid, it experiences an upward force that is equal to the weight of the fluid displaced by it.
When a body is immersed fully or partially in a fluid, it experiences a downward force that is equal to the weight of the fluid displaced by it.
When a body is immersed fully or partially in a fluid, it experiences a downward force that is much less than the weight of the fluid displaced by it.

q: The calculated formula unit mass of Na2SO4 is:
If resistors are connected in series, the total or equivalent resistance is ________.
o: Always less than any single resistor

q: Which of the following substances is NOT an example of biodegradable substance?
Propanone belongs to which homologous series?
Why do the manufacturers of chips flush chips bags with nitrogen before packing?
Which of the following statements correctly defines the principal axis of a spherical mirror?
o: The perpendicular line drawn from the pole
The line passing through the focus and object
The line passing through the centre of curvature and pole

q: Which of the following tissues have contractile proteins that contract and relax to cause movement?
What is an ion in chemistry?
What are the unwanted impurities such as soil and sand present in an ore called?
Which of the following substances have a high chance of contributing to the phenomenon of biological magnification?

A smooth ball is resting on an ideal frictionless plane inclined on both sides. The ball is released from the left and the angle of right plane inclination is small in Case-I and zero in Case-II (flat). The ball travels ________ compared to the equally inclined case.
o: equal distance in Case-I and farther distance in Case-II
equal distance in Case-I and moves forever in Case-II
farther distance in Case-I and moves forever in Case-II
small distance in Case-I and equal distance in Case-II

q: Consider the following statements and select the correct option. Statement A: Salts of strong acids and strong bases are neutral with pH 7. Statement B: Sodium chloride is an example of a neutral salt.
o: Both statements A and B are incorrect.
Statement A is correct but B is incorrect.
Both statements A and B are correct.
Statement A is incorrect but B is correct.

q: The angle between the incident ray and the emergent ray from a prism is called
Arrange the following schemes in the ascending order of their discounts. A. Buy 3 get 2 free. B. A single discount of 65%. C. Two successive discounts of 31% and 40%.
If 20% of a number is added to 72, then the result is the same number. 60% of the same number is:

Two banks, A and B, offered loans at 3.5% and 7% per annum, respectively. Keshav borrowed an amount of Rs.4,80,000 from each bank. Find the positive difference (in Rs.) between the amounts of simple interest paid to the two banks by Keshav after 2 years.
P is any point inside the rectangle ABCD. If PA = 86 cm, PB = 49 cm and PC = 79 cm, then the length of PD (in cm) is equal to:
A bus covers a distance of 1,200 km in 3 hours. What is the speed of the bus?
Amit and Shikha together started a business by investing Rs.46,800. At the end of the year, the total profit was Rs.8,750. If Amit's share of profit is Rs.2,900, then what was the investment of Amit?

A class of 69 students took a Physics test. 17 students had an average score of 94. The other students had an average score of 84. What is the average score (rounded off to one decimal place) of the whole class?

A cistern of dimensions 100 cm x 80 cm x 120 cm is fitted with an inlet pipe and an outlet pipe. The inlet pipe pours in 2000 cm^3 of water per minute and the outlet pipe can empty the full tank in 3 hours. If the tank is full and both pipes are open, how many hours will it take to empty the cistern?
If 30 men and 5 women can complete a piece of work in 84140 days, then in how many days will 55 men and 18 women complete the same work?
If the price of a product is first increased by 18% and then decreased by 22%, find the percentage change in the price (correct to two places of decimals).
The marked price of a bed is Rs.744, which is 36% above the cost price. If the profit percentage is 20%, find the discount percentage (rounded off to two decimal places).

The ratio of the price per unit of lemon chocolate to that of strawberry chocolate is 22 : 6, and a family consumes them in the ratio of 14 : 23 by quantity. Determine the ratio of the expenditure on strawberry chocolate to that on lemon chocolate.
The average age of 25 students of a class is 27 years. If the teacher's age is included, the average age of the whole class increases to 28 years. What is the age of the teacher (in years)?

A solid sphere of radius 7 cm is coated with a layer of paint that increases its radius to 7.5 cm, find the volume of the painted layer. (Use pi = 22/7 and round off your answer to the nearest integer)

A hollow metallic sphere with an external radius of 10 cm and an internal radius of 6 cm is melted and recast into a solid cone with a base radius of 8 cm. What is the height of the cone? (Use pi = 22/7)
